event.summary: Recorded passing validation; acceptance remains a separate explicit transition.

acceptance.proof: The projection borrows accepted K state, constructs exactly one seven-node info tree under caller-selected accepted limits, preserves the source, and delegates every XML value/tree operation to accepted B through E contracts.

All seven accepted case inputs execute directly in Rust and produce the exact six static leaf names, represented values, direct-child order, and compact G serialization expected from accepted H/K/L evidence.

Unsupported regular rates reject before XML composition, and copied-value, XML text, character-data, tree, and serialization bounds retain typed indexed failures without fallback policy or partial accepted state.

The local overlay labels compact static element serialization as candidate output only and does not claim the observed XML declaration, whitespace, desc form/content, volatile fields, complete info document, endpoint bytes, or runtime behavior.
Cargo files, dependency closure, feature lock, runtime effects, external oracle, networking, adapters, providers, devices, and Manifold authority remain unchanged and inert.

Public instructions route the static XML composition and its no-desc/no-volatile/no-document/no-runtime limits; shared skills are reviewed; the portable workspace remains fail-closed with no device requirement.

document.commit_policy: Commit the bounded static info element-tree projection separately from desc composition, observed document whitespace/declaration policy, volatile/runtime fields, protocol, transport, adapters, providers, features, or external integration.

instruction_surfaces.change_reason: Route the bounded static info element composition, exact six-child order, compact local serialization, and strict no-desc/no-volatile/no-runtime boundary.
Describe the static XML composition surface, accepted evidence reuse, typed delegation failures, and remaining description/document/runtime work.
Review whether the static-element versus complete-document boundary adds reusable contract guidance; retain the skill unchanged if current artifact-role and authority rules cover it.
instruction_surfaces.validation: Review the installed skill and run its instruction-surface whitespace check.

instruction_surfaces.change_reason: Review whether the clean Rusty LSL static XML surface needs new repo-family routing; retain the skill unchanged if current public/source/Manifold boundaries cover it.
instruction_surfaces.validation: Review the installed skill and run its instruction-surface whitespace check.

non_scope: Any change to Cargo.toml, Cargo.lock, morphospace/feature.lock.json, morphospace/project.spec.json, existing accepted fixtures, oracle drivers, raw captures, H/K/L observations or provenance, dependencies, Cargo features, unsafe Rust, generated protocol material, or native libraries.

XML declaration, inter-element whitespace, self-closing tags, attributes, namespaces, schemas, canonicalization, parsing, decoding, round trips, endpoint bytes, or a complete stream-info document compatibility claim.
Generic metadata-to-desc interpretation or composition, desc placement or empty form, channel metadata conventions, or mutation of the accepted generic MetadataTree.
Version, created_at, uid, session_id, hostname, addresses, ports, or any other volatile/runtime-populated or transport-owned field.
Broad floating-point lexical compatibility outside the accepted LSLC-001L domain, or any fallback formatting policy.

Oracle execution, network access, source-repository implementation inspection, or use of liblsl, rLSL, pylsl, wrapper, application, vendored, generated, protocol, or test source as implementation input.

Discovery, resolution, inlet, outlet, samples, chunks, buffering, queues, clocks, correction, recovery, protocol, wire, sockets, networking, runtime activation, FFI, adapters, providers, devices, Quest, Hostess, or platform lifecycle.
Manifold stream admission, registry, subscription, route, lease, revision, replay, identity, authorization, audit, command, session, topology, transport, or product-policy authority.
document.objective: Project the accepted six-role StreamInfoStaticFields view and LSLC-001L numeric spellings into one dependency-free bounded XmlElementTree rooted at info, preserving the observed static child order and accepted character-data policy while adding no desc, volatile/runtime field, complete-document, protocol, transport, provider, device, feature, or authority behavior.
outputs: A bounded public projection from one borrowed accepted StreamInfoStaticFields view into one owned XmlElementTree with root info and exactly six direct leaf children in name, type, channel_count, channel_format, source_id, nominal_srate order.

Typed indexed delegation errors for numeric spelling, exact fallible copied-text/name allocation, XML text, character-data, element-tree, and compact serialization validation without consuming or mutating the source definition.

Direct Rust execution of all seven accepted LSLC-001H/K/L cases, including exact static compact serialization through the unchanged LSLC-001G serializer and fail-closed rejection of unsupported regular rates and undersized limits.

A separate LSLC-001M local-results overlay and focused checker binding the accepted H, K, L, and G evidence while leaving observed public XML, desc, volatile fields, and full-document candidate status unchanged.
